_status: Query-diversity axes for Stage 3, following DataMorgana (Filice et al. 2025, arXiv:2501.12789 — READ in full). NB the paper's ablation (Tables 4-5) shows the persona/'user categorization' axis contributes only MARGINALLY to diversity; phrasing/premise/terminology (their 'linguistic-variation') carry it. Persona is KEPT here for audience-realism (this tool serves clinician/bioinformatician/student, unlike generic QA) and is under a planned on/off ablation (EXPERIMENTS.md Exp 13). These control the NATURAL-LANGUAGE query only; the config is deterministic from the KB and never touched by the teacher. Sample one category per axis, weighted by 'probability'.

phrasing.name: verbose-natural
phrasing.description: a short paragraph with extra context

premise.name: explicit
premise.description: state species, assay and cohort explicitly in the query

premise.name: implicit
premise.description: leave some context implied by the wording

persona.name: clinician
persona.description: a clinical geneticist preparing a diagnostic report

persona.name: student
persona.description: a graduate student learning the tool

terminology.name: field-standard
terminology.description: use field terms (WES, SNV, AF, MANE)

terminology.name: lay
terminology.description: use plain-language equivalents (exome sequencing, spelling change, how common)
